title: Temporal Human-State Testable Reference Implementation
description: Productionize temporal human state as the first complete consumer of the Sprint 5 transaction and approval-context foundation, with a fully automatable TEST lane and separately governed PRODUCTION lane.
acceptance_criteria: Human-state observation, transition, policy, revocation, and model-feedback operations are journaled, recoverable, idempotent, and receipted
TEST-scoped consent, privacy, correction, transition, revocation, and model-use approvals can exercise the full automated human-state loop without external side effects
PRODUCTION human-state and model-use actions require recognized governing authority and cannot consume TEST approvals
Privacy classification, consent, purpose scope, redaction, expiration, and revocation fail closed in both execution contexts
CLI commands, canonical collections, generated views, authority manifest, and forensic audit preserve execution context
Behavior fixtures prove correct treatment of current, contextual, uncertain, disputed, retracted, superseded, and context-inapplicable state
